rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Claims Ccp 437c(F)(1)
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 27, 2025 Line 1. RESPONDENT LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Claims Ccp 437c(F)(1). Respondent La Galleria Condominium Owners' Association's motion for summary adjudication of Petitioner Joseph Dick's first cause of action is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Third Party Early Warning Services, Llc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 27, 2025 Line 11. PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI's Motion To Compel Third Party Early Warning Services, Llc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents. Plaintiff Charles Li' s Motion To Compel Third Party Early Warning Services, LLC To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The courtesy copy of Plaintiff's moving papers do not include copies of the EWS subpoena or the consumer objections with his moving papers, or any of the other attachments to the Thai Declaration, thus the court is not able to necessary review. Further, the proof of service does not show that the moving papers were served on all parties to the action. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUERAU, INC. VS. GT HAULING LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 27, 2025 Line 2. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUERAU, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc.'s motion to Defendant GT Hauling LLC's answer to the complaint is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The motion is premised on Defendant's status as a corporation without counsel in the action, given the court authorized Defendant's counsel's withdraw on December 23, 2024. But, in fact, counsel Brian Horn and Brightwork Law PC remain counsel of record for Defendant GT Hauling LLC. The court never signed an order granting counsel's request to withdraw (likely because counsel did not submit an order following the hearing). And, in any event, an order allowing counsel to withdraw is not effective until served and, here, no such order could have been served. These facts also make plain the instant motion was not properly served. Plaintiff served Defendant GT Hauling LLC, but not Defendant's counsel of record. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike All Pending Motions & Oppositions Filed By Defendant Qoo10 Pte Ltd For Lack Of Standing And Judicial Economy
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, March 27, 2025 Line 6. PLAINTIFF GENOA MARTELL's Motion To Strike All Pending Motions & Oppositions Filed By Defendant Qoo10 Pte Ltd For Lack Of Standing And Judicial Economy. Plaintiff Genoa Martell's Motion To Strike All Pending Motions & Oppositions Filed By Defendant Qoo10 Pte Ltd For Lack Of Standing And Judicial Economy is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion fails under CCP section 436 because she did not meet and confer, not does she provide a factual or legal basis 436 for striking the papers. The court declines to exercise its authority under CCP 128(a)(3) to strike any of the papers specified by Plaintiff. The discovery statutes, including the sanctions provisions, are entirely inapposite to a motion to strike. Plaintiff's request for an order directing Defendant Qoo10 to provide certain information and documents is without legal or factual support. The California Civil Discovery Act provides the means for a party to obtain material information from another. Plaintiff is admonished to review and follow the Civil Discovery Act and associated rules. Plaintiff's last-minute request to withdraw this motion-submitted one court day before the hearing-so that she can later file a subsequent motion seeking substantially similar relief is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
